raw:

Slow Cooker Tajín Chicken and Peppers
By Sarah DiGregorio
Published April 24, 2025
Media 1 of 1
Total Time
About 4 hours
Prep Time
5 minutes
Cook Time
4 hours
5
(1,457)
Read 199 comments
Give
Ingredients
4 servings
Ingredients
2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken thighs
2 red bell peppers, sliced
1 yellow onion, sliced
2 tablespoons Tajín
1 teaspoon kosher salt
Add ingredients to Grocery List
Nutritional Information
Preparation
Step 1
Add the chicken, peppers, onion, Tajín and salt to a slow cooker.
Step 2
Cover and cook on low until the chicken is tender, about 4 hours.
Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.
https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1026792-slow-cooker-tajin-chicken-and-peppers?campaign_id=90

name: Facebook reel copy starts with profile name and has numbered method without heading
raw:
https://www.facebook.com/reel/1587189429597979
Coconut and Bliss

 ·
Follow
﻿origins · sweet love
Crispy red curry chicken bites - they’re basically little rice paper dumplings, cooked in the air fryer until crispy 
Ingredients
500g chicken mince
2 tbsp red curry paste
1 tbsp soy sauce
1 tbsp honey
1/2 lime, juiced
1/2 tbsp grated ginger
1 garlic clove, grated
2 tbsp chives, finely chopped
8 rice paper wrappers
Oil spray
Dipping Sauce
1/3 cup Kewpie mayonnaise
1 tbsp red curry paste
1 tbsp lime juice
1 tsp honey
1 tsp soy sauce
1 tsp rice wine vinegar
To Serve
Chopped coriander
Chilli crisp
1. In a bowl, combine the chicken mince, red curry paste, soy sauce, honey, lime juice, ginger, garlic, chives  until well mixed.
2. Fill a shallow bowl with warm water. Dip one rice paper wrapper into the water until just softened.
3. Spoon a log of the chicken mixture along the bottom third of the wrapper. Fold in the sides and roll tightly into a sausage shape.
4. Using kitchen scissors, snip the roll into bite-sized pieces.
5. Arrange the pieces in a single layer in an air fryer basket. Spray generously with oil and air fry at 200°C for 12–15 minutes, turning halfway, until the chicken is cooked through and the rice paper is golden and crispy.
6. Meanwhile, whisk together the Kewpie mayonnaise, red curry paste, lime juice, honey, soy sauce and rice wine vinegar until smooth.
7. Top the crispy chicken bites with chopped coriander and chilli crisp, then serve with the dipping sauce. See less

expected.title: Crispy red curry chicken bites - they’re basically little rice paper dumplings, cooked in the air fryer until crispy
expected.author: Coconut and Bliss

Arrange the pieces in a single layer in an air fryer basket. Spray generously with oil and air fry at 200°C for 12–15 minutes, turning halfway, until the chicken is cooked through and the rice paper is golden and crispy.
Meanwhile, whisk together the Kewpie mayonnaise, red curry paste, lime juice, honey, soy sauce and rice wine vinegar until smooth.
Top the crispy chicken bites with chopped coriander and chilli crisp, then serve with the dipping sauce.

Soy-baked potatoes with tuna sriracha mayo
4 soy-baked potatoes with tuna sriracha mayo
Soy-baked potatoes with tuna sriracha mayo
Fliss Freeborn
Serves 4
Easy
Prep:
10 mins
Cook:
15 mins
A star rating of 4.6 out of 5.
18 ratings
Rate
3 comments
Serve jacket potatoes with a spicy mayo that’s fragrant with ginger for a fresh take on the usual cheesy beans number. They're perfect for a speedy supper
Low calorie
Low fat
Try our app
Showing items 1 to 3 of 6

Ingredients
Nutrition
4 large baking potatoes
3 x 135g cans tuna
chunks in brine, drained
2 tbsp mayonnaise
2 ½ tbsp natural yogurt
4 spring onions
thinly sliced
1 thumb-sized piece of ginger
peeled and finely grated
1 lemon
juiced
2 tsp sriracha
or hot sauce
3 pickled gherkins
finely chopped (optional)
2 tbsp reduced-salt soy sauce
320g spinach
For the dressing
1 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
2 tbsp cider vinegar
¼ tsp wholegrain mustard
Keep the screen awake with cook mode on the Good Food app.
Ad


Method
step 1
Prick the potatoes all over using a fork, then microwave on high for 10-15 mins, or until softened. Heat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6, or turn on the air-fryer.

step 2
Tip the tuna into a bowl and mix with the mayo, yogurt, spring onions, ginger, half the lemon juice, the hot sauce and gherkins, if using.

step 3
When the potatoes are tender, score them lightly with a sharp knife, then pour over the soy sauce and toss quickly to coat. Bake in the oven for 15-20 mins or the air-fryer at 200C for 5 mins to crisp up the skins. Meanwhile, combine the dressing ingredients with the remaining lemon juice and drizzle this over the spinach. Split the potatoes in half, fill with the tuna mayo and serve with the spinach.

https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/soy-baked-potatoes-with-tuna-sriracha-mayo

expected.title: Soy-baked potatoes with tuna sriracha mayo

name: Facebook reel with several ingredients collapsed onto single lines and a calories-per-portion macro line
raw:
Alex's Kitchen Bangers

 ·
Following
﻿MoBlack · Yamore (Edit Version)
Red Thai Curry Chicken Skewers
If you’re looking for your new favourite summer dinner… you’ve just found it 🤭
Red Thai curry marinated chicken skewers with jasmine rice, crunchy salad and the dreamiest creamy coconut red Thai sauce 🤤
So easy to make and perfect for when you need a quick & absolutely banging dinner 🤌🏼
Ingredients (serves 2):
Chicken Skewers
300g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, diced into large chunks (can use breast for lower cal/ higher protein) 50g 0% Greek style yoghurt 30g red Thai curry paste 1 tsp garlic purée 1 tsp ginger purée Juice of ½ lime
Red Thai Sauce
30g red Thai curry paste 100ml light coconut milk 1 tsp light soy sauce ½ tsp honey ½ tsp fish sauce Juice of ½ lime
To Serve
1 x 250g pouch microwave jasmine rice
 ½ cucumber, diced
1 medium carrot, julienned or ribboned Juice of ½ lime Pinch of salt 2-3 sprigs fresh coriander, chopped
💞In a bowl combine all of the skewer ingredients.  Cover and marinate for at least 30 minutes, or overnight, then thread onto skewers.
💞Air fry at 200°C for 12-14 minutes, turning halfway through, until lightly charred and cooked through.
💞Toss the carrot with the lime juice and a pinch of salt. Set aside while the chicken cooks.
💞Heat the jasmine rice according to the packet instructions.
💞Add the red Thai curry paste to a small saucepan over a medium heat and cook for 1 minute. Add the honey, light soy & fish sauce and for a further 30 seconds before stirring in the coconut milk. Simmer until smooth and slightly thickened, then remove from the heat and stir through the lime juice.
💞Divide the rice between two bowls. Arrange the cucumber and carrot alongside the rice before laying the skewers over the top.
💞Drizzle over the red thai sauce and finish with the chopped coriander and an extra squeeze of fresh lime.
Calories per portion: 600 | Protein: 34.9g | Fat: 24.8g | Carbs: 58.8g
https://www.facebook.com/reel/1425719482743903

expected.title: Red Thai Curry Chicken Skewers
expected.author: Alex's Kitchen Bangers
